weatherForecast.comment:
During the night, the sky will initially be partly cloudy, with increasing cloud cover from the east after midnight.
On Wednesday, dense clouds will frequently restrict visibility at first and it may snow a little in places. During the daytime changes, it will become somewhat friendlier, with better visibility and occasional sunshine. Strong east to north-easterly winds with gusts of around 70 km/h at high altitudes. Temperatures during the day changes to between -7 and -3 degrees at 2000 metres and between -12 and -9 degrees at 3000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Beware of fresh wind slab, avalanche prone locations in persistent weak layers are rare but have serious consequences
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is considerable above the tree line and low below.
Avalanches in wind slabs can be triggered very easily by individuals, in particular in the aspects from west to north to east. Danger areas are located behind terrain edges as well as in steep gullies and bowls and in the forest boundary area. Avalanches can reach medium size, and avalanches can occasionally become large by tearing through deeper weak layers in the persistent weak layer.
Above 2000 m, in the aspects from west to north to east, medium and occasionally large, dry slab avalanches can still be triggered by individuals in persistent weak layers in a few places. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but often not recognisable in the terrain. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow.
In sunny conditions, small loose snow avalanches can usually be triggered from extremely steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: 30 to 40 cm of new fallen snow fell on Tuesday. New and wind slab snow lies in shady high areas on soft, faceted snow. Away from this, the snow has fallen on a melt-freeze crust. Deeper in the snowpack, faceted crystals can be found on a crust; close to the ground, the snowpack consists of angular, cup-shaped crystals. The connection at the transition to the deep snowpack near the ground is still poor. Deep and sunny middle layers were largely covered with snow before the snowfall.

tendency.highlights: Gradual settlement of the snowdrift accumulations.

weatherForecast.comment:
During the night, the sky will initially be partly cloudy, with increasing cloud cover from the east after midnight.
On Wednesday, dense clouds will frequently restrict visibility at first and it may snow a little in places. During the daytime changes, it will become somewhat friendlier, with better visibility and occasional sunshine. Strong east to north-easterly winds with gusts of around 70 km/h at high altitudes. Temperatures during the day changes to between -7 and -3 degrees at 2000 metres and between -12 and -9 degrees at 3000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Pay attention to fresh wind slab, avalanche prone locations in persistent weak layers are rare but have serious consequences
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is moderate above the tree line and low below.
In the aspects from west to north to south-east, avalanches can easily be triggered by individuals in wind slabs. Danger areas are located behind terrain edges as well as in steep gullies and bowls and in the forest boundary area. Avalanches can occasionally reach medium size, and avalanches can occasionally become large when tearing through deeper weak layers in the persistent weak layer.
Above 2000 m, in the aspects from west to north to east, medium and occasionally large, dry slab avalanches can still be triggered by individuals in a few places. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but often not recognisable in the terrain. Take particular care at the transitions from little to more snow.
In sunlight, small loose snow avalanches can usually be triggered from extremely steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: 15 to 30 cm of new fallen snow fell on Tuesday. New and wind slab snow lies in shady high areas on soft, faceted snow. Away from this, the snow has fallen on a melt-freeze crust. Deeper in the snowpack, faceted crystals can be found on a crust; close to the ground, the snowpack consists of angular, cup-shaped crystals. The connection at the transition to the deep snowpack near the ground is still poor. Deep and sunny middle layers were largely snowed out before the snowfall.

tendency.highlights: Gradual settlement of the snowdrift accumulations.

weatherForecast.comment:
During the night, the sky will initially be partly cloudy, with increasing cloud cover from the east after midnight.
On Wednesday, dense clouds will frequently restrict visibility at first and it may snow a little in places. During the daytime changes, it will become somewhat friendlier, with better visibility and occasional sunshine. Strong east to north-easterly winds with gusts of around 50 km/h at high altitudes. Temperatures during the day changes to between -7 and -3 degrees at 2000 metres and between -12 and -9 degrees at 3000 metres.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Low risk of isolated wet snow avalanches
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche risk is low.
Above the tree line, small slab avalanches can be triggered in places on steep terrain adjacent to ridgelines with little additional load. In addition, in the entrance areas of steep gullies on shady slopes with a high additional load, small slabs of snow can also be triggered occasionally. The risk of being swept away and falling on icy surfaces that may be thinly covered with snow and therefore difficult to recognise generally outweighs the risk of burial.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new and wind slab snow lies on a mostly well-settled old snowpack. Weak layers of overblown new fallen snow near the surface can be seen on a small scale. In the higher on shady slopes, there are still weak layers of large faceted crystals in the lower part of the snowpack, especially near the ground.
Low and sunny mid-altitude areas were largely snowed out before the snowfall.

tendency.highlights: No significant change in avalanche danger.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Fresh, prone to triggering wind slab and weak persistent weak layer
